title: equisetum × trachyodon var. moorei
intro: Equisetum × trachyodon var. moorei, also known as Moore's horsetail, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetum genus and is a hybrid of Equisetum telmateia and Equisetum limosum. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and arthritis. The health benefits of Moore's horsetail include its ability to improve kidney function, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory issues. Traditionally, Moore's horsetail is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
